message:
fix(storage): remove redundant FTS5 DDL from fresh schema, propagate FTS insert errors for transactional consistency

Two related fixes to the FTS5 contentless migration (V14):

1. Remove stale CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS fts_messages DDL
   from MIGRATION_FRESH_SCHEMA. After the V14 migration the FTS5 table
   is created by the migration chain, not the fresh schema block. The
   redundant DDL was harmless but confusing.

2. Make per-row FTS fallback inserts propagate errors instead of
   silently skipping failed rows. Previously, when a batch INSERT
   failed and fell back to per-row inserts, individual row failures
   were logged and skipped — allowing canonical rows (messages table)
   and search index rows (fts_messages) to diverge silently.

   Now the per-row fallback uses .with_context()? to abort the
   transaction on any FTS write failure, ensuring canonical and index
   rows stay consistent. The batch_error message is captured once and
   included in the per-row error context for debugging.

Tests (tests/storage.rs):
- insert_conversation_tree_rolls_back_when_fts_insert_fails: drops
  fts_messages, verifies single-conversation insert aborts and rolls
  back both conversations and messages tables
- insert_conversations_batched_rolls_back_when_fts_insert_fails: same
  pattern for the batched insert path with multiple conversations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
